global.title: Ship drive-level collaboration first, document-level scoping comes in a later milestone
global.content:
## Decision: drive-level first, document-level later

### Phase 1 — drive-level collaboration (first ship)

- Simpler mental model: "I'm collaborating on this whole drive with Alice and Bob."
- Covers the drive ops (`ADD_FOLDER`, `MOVE_NODE`, `ADD_FILE`) and every doc inside the drive.
- A **single ACT grant per participant** covers the drive manifest, all child doc manifests, and all op batches — minimizing ACT chain management.

### Phase 2 — document-level collaboration

- Narrower: "I'm collaborating on this one doc, but keep the rest of the drive private."
- ACT grantee list is scoped to one doc's manifest and op batches only.
- The drive manifest itself is **not** shared — participants see a free-standing doc, not its parent drive.
- Useful when the containing drive has confidential material and only one doc is meant for outside eyes.

Drive-level first is the right build order because the mental model is simpler for users, a single ACT scope is easier to reason about, and it still exercises every layer of the mesh (manifest, peer feeds, GSOC, signatures). Document-level scoping then narrows the ACT chain without changing the plumbing.
global.description: Drive-level collab ships first (simpler mental model, single ACT grant covers all children); document-level scoping comes in a later milestone.
